summary: Set 030 Session 3: Phase 3 reader migration + Explorer label alignment per spec D13 + D3. Added read_progress/readProgress wrappers to both progress helpers as the canonical reader entry point; migrated every application reader in ai_router/ (close-out gates check_activity_log_entry / check_next_orchestrator_present / check_change_log_fresh, start_session preflight, close_session _peek_session_number, __init__.py cost reporter) and the TS extension (fileSystem.ts readSessionSets state-file block + isMidSetComplete rewritten as a v3-invariant probe + new readClosedSessionsFromLedger v2-compat helper, SessionSetsProvider.ts isCurrentSessionInFlight null-check + Explorer label 'Done' -> 'Complete' + ICON_FILES key rename). SessionState TypeScript union literal 'done' -> 'complete' across types.ts and 24 downstream test fixtures. TS lazy-synth writers (sessionState.ts notStartedPayload + backfillPayload) updated to mirror Python Session 2's v3 dual-write shape via new buildSessions helper. Added two D13 lint guards (Python pytest + Mocha) that scan for direct legacy-field dict-access patterns and allowlist progress.py + writers + close_session._run_repair (v2-compat carve-out) + tests + lines with '# noqa: D13' / '// noqa: D13'. Round A verification (gpt-5-4, $0.28155) found 1 must-fix: backfillPayload could emit reader-invalid {status: complete, no sessions[]} snapshots when spec.md lacks totalSessions; fixed on both TS and Python sides (fall through to not-started shape when buildSessions returns undefined) + 3 regression tests. Round B verification (gpt-5-4, $0.23331) VERIFIED all 4 Round A scope items line-by-line and surfaced 1 NEW finding (strict-vs-defensive handling of malformed v2 fields in synthesize_v3_from_v2) — deliberately deferred per memory feedback_verifier_spiral_recruit_codex (the filter behavior was explicitly approved by the Session 1 verifier; the new finding is a strict-vs-defensive philosophy debate not a regression). Tests: pytest 538 passed + 1 skipped + 8 e2e deselected (+9 from new D13 lint tests + Round-A regression tests + read_progress wrapper tests); Mocha unit 376 passing + 2 failing (both pre-existing baseline failures unrelated to v3); Layer 3 Playwright 5/5 passing against v3 fixtures (Complete bucket label, N/N Complete annotation, force-closed buckets to Complete with [FORCED] badge per v3 force-promotes-all semantic from Session 2); tsc --noEmit clean. Operator-visible behavior changes: (1) Explorer label 'Done' -> 'Complete'; (2) v2 bare {status: complete} snapshots without per-session evidence now bucket as In Progress (Session 4 bulk migrator heals them); (3) force-closed sets now bucket as Complete with [FORCED] badge (was In Progress with badge under v2).

reason.specifics: Session 4 ships the bulk migrator CLI (python -m ai_router.migrate_session_state) and runs it against the 28+ in-repo state files to flip them from v2 to v3 dual-write shape. Also includes the Lightweight-tier ergonomics dry-run against a representative dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb state file (per spec D16) and the consumer-repo dry-run docs (docs/migration-v3-dry-run.md). Builds a v0.14.0-rc.1 VSIX for internal smoke-test; NO publish (publishing moves to Session 5 per spec D14 revision, after the in-extension migration UX is in place). Keeping Claude Opus 4.7 @ effort=high preserves the architectural context of how the v3 invariants interact with the bulk-migration UX (which the Session 5 in-extension lazy migrator will share).
